body:
This PR simplifies the entrainment detrainment logic, so I can work easier with the code. 

Previously the computations were split over many different functions that were only used once. There is no reason to define them separately. Also the input model variables were packed into a separate object with some name changes along the way. That was also confusing.

I'm about to be adding different detrainment options and it will help me to have all detrainment physics happening in one place.

Also, this is just refactoring. The CI cases should be the same
title: entrainment_detrainment cleanup

body:
The performance monitoring is a bit messy because it's a bit too heavily dependent on `parsed_args_per_job_id`.

This PR reorganizes the performance jobs:
 - Collects them all under `Performance` in the pipeline
 - Defines the model config in `perf/config_parsed_args.jl`
 - Unifies perf config scrips to use `perf/config_parsed_args.jl`
 - Specifies parameters by precedence:
    - Highest precedence: args defined in `ARGS`
    - Mid     precedence: args defined in `parsed_args_perf_target` (below)
    - Lowest  precedence: args defined in `cli_defaults(s)`
  - Adds a flame graph for a job with edmf on a sphere

This makes the perf monitoring configuration a bit more flat-- all jobs will now depend on the config defined in `perf/config_parsed_args.jl` using the described precedence.

Supersedes #1166.
title: Reorganize perf jobs

body: This PR swaps `map` for `ntuple` in the TC kernel. It improves inference and preps for GPU-readiness. It also happens to trim allocations by 20%.
title: Use ntuple in TC kernel

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
As most of the ci jobs only test whether the configuration works, this PR tries to reduce the total number of jobs. See `Content` for the details. cc @bischtob 

## To-do
<!---  list of proposed tasks in the PR, move to "Content" on completion 
- Proposed task
-->


## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->
ci:
- Removes `sphere_baroclinic_wave_rhoe_Float64`: we test Float64 in some other experiments
- Changes `sphere_held_suarez_rhoe` to `sphere_held_suarez_rhoe_hightop` with 45km domain top
- Removes `sphere_held_suarez_rhoe_equilmoist`: we have a 45km domain top of this `sphere_held_suarez_rhoe_equilmoist_hightop_sponge`
- Removes `sphere_held_suarez_rhoe_equilmoist_mo`: we test monin-obukhov in some other experiments
- Removes all the aquaplanet simulations (except the EDMF one) and combines them into one job `sphere_aquaplanet_rhoe_equilmoist_allsky_gw_hightop`, which contains moisture, 0M microphysics, all-sky radiation, nonorographic and graphic gravity waves, sponges, and 45km domain top
  - Changes all held-suarez and aquaplanet simulations to 45km domain top, except for the one with EDMF, as I am not sure if that works
- Removes `mpi_baroclinic_wave_rhoe`: we test mpi on aquaplanet simulations
- Removes `no_surface`: `surface_scheme` is default to nothing

longrun:
- Removes `longrun_hs_rhoe_highres`: we have a stable `longrun_hs_rhoe_highres_hightop`
- Removes `longrun_hs_rhoe_equil_highres_monin_obukhov_Float64`: we test monin obukhov in other experiments
- Removes `longrun_aquaplanet_rhoe_equil_allsky` and `longrun_aquaplanet_rhoe_equil_allsky_topography`: we don't really care about these low resolution runs, and we don't care much about the stability all-sky radiation now. The ci makes sure all-sky radiation works.
